title: Method for manufacturing a back lighted display panel
abstract: A method for manufacturing a translucent display panel (1), wherein said panel is formed as a sandwich structure (2), wherein said sandwich structure (2) comprises a substrate sheet material (3), a first (4') and second (4") adhesive layer (4) and a first (5') and second (5") surface structure layer (5), wherein the method comprises the steps of: providing a substrate sheet material (3), having a top (6) and bottom (7) side; providing a first (4') and second (4") adhesive layer onto the top (6) and bottom (7) side of the substrate sheet material (3); and providing a first (5') and second (5") surface structure layer (5) onto said first (4') and second (4") adhesive layer respectively; thereby forming a sandwich structure precursor (2'), wherein said sandwich structure precursor (2') is subsequently pressed in a calender (10) to form the sandwich structure (2).
claims: Claims 1 . A method for manufacturing a translucent display panel (1 ), wherein said panel is formed as a sandwich structure (2), wherein said sandwich structure (2) comprises a substrate sheet material (3), a first (4') and second (4") adhesive layer (4) and a first (5') and second (5") surface structure layer (5), wherein the method comprises the steps of: providing a substrate sheet material (3), having a top (6) and bottom (7) side; providing a first (4') and second (4") adhesive layer onto the top (6) and bottom (7) side of the substrate sheet material (3); and providing a first (5') and second (5") surface structure layer (5) onto said first (4') and second (4") adhesive layer respectively; thereby forming a sandwich structure precursor (2') characterized in that said sandwich structure precursor (2') is subsequently pressed in a calender (10) to form the sandwich structure (2). 2. The method as claimed in claim 1 , wherein said calender operates at a pressure (P) from 15 to 55 kg/cm 2 , or from 20 to 50 kg/cm 2 , or from 25 to 45 kg/cm 2 . 3. The method as claimed in claim 1 , wherein said substrate sheet material (2) is a paper sheet material, wherein said paper sheet material is a dry coated paper. 4. The method as claimed in claim 1 , wherein said adhesive layer is a hot-setting adhesive material. 5. The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein said adhesive layer is any one of a polyethylene (PE), a low-density polyethylene (LDPE) material and a polypropylene (PP) material. 6. The method as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the surface structure layer comprise a dimensionally stable material. 7. The method as claimed in claim 6, wherein the surface structure layer comprise a polyester (PE) material. 8. The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein said adhesive layer is provided onto said substrate sheet material by means of extrusion. 9. The method as claimed in claim 8, wherein the adhesive is fed at a rate in a range of from 5 to 60 g/m 2 , or in a range of from 5 to 50 g/m 2 , or in the range of from 5 to 40 g/m 2 , or in a range from 10 to 35 g/m 2 . 10. The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein said surface structure layer is provided as a film. 1 1 .The method as claimed in claim 10, wherein the film has a thickness in the range of 10 to 25 micrometers, or in the range of 15 to 20 micrometers. 12. A translucent display panel (1 ) obtainable by the method as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 1 1 . 13. The use of the translucent display panel (1 ) as claimed in claim 12 for printing. 14. The use of the translucent display panel (1 ) as claimed in claim 12 as an advertising or informational display panel, wherein said panel is back lighted.
